notes: Requires graph3 module with 3D surface rendering, lighting, and perspective projection
cylinder() now returns revolution with _grid; draw(revolution,pen) renders silhouette outline with hidden-line dashing
Implement gamma function (Lanczos approximation) to enable rendering of condor parametric surface
Implemented 3D Bounds axis rendering with bounding box, tick marks, and axis labels. SSIM improved but valley shading differs from reference due to two-sided lighting needed for other diagrams.
Single-point paths from empty guide concatenation (g--point) returned empty due to points.length<2 check. Fixed by marking with _singlePoint and treating as pair during inline path building.
Label-dominated layout scaling removed; exRatio adjusted from 0.5 to 0.34 to better match TeXeR font metrics

Fixed MathJax red-to-correct-color issue for unrecognized commands like \extdollar. Color now renders correctly (green instead of red). However, SSIM remains low (0.33) due to fundamental size differences between MathJax and TeXeR rendering. MathJax output is ~19% wider (993px vs 837px). Attempts to route single unknown commands to plain SVG text failed because renderLabelWithScripts doesn't preserve backslash characters. Next attempt should consider: (1) adjusting MathJax width scaling factor globally (risky for other diagrams), (2) implementing backslash-preserving plain text rendering for unrecognized commands, or (3) accepting this as a rendering engine limitation where structural content is correct but metrics differ.

Fixed MathJax red-to-correct-color issue for unrecognized commands. Color now correct (green vs red). SSIM low (0.33) due to MathJax vs TeXeR size differences (993px vs 837px width). Color fix improves SSIM +0.002 and visual correctness. No canary regression. Next attempt: implement backslash-preserving plain text render for unrecognized commands, or accept as rendering engine limitation.
Increased minReasonable threshold from 50bp to 100bp for very small unitsize (<10bp/unit) to ensure dense label diagrams remain readable

Strip corrupted TAB byte from label text instead of converting it to backslash. TeXeR treats TAB as whitespace; the previous heuristic produced bogus commands like \extdollar / \heta that MathJax rendered in red. Fix lifted target 0.363 -> 0.774 and produced large family-wide wins (02868: 0.01->0.97, 08898: 0.38->0.94, 09481-09485 all up 0.02-0.41). | VERIFIER-ERROR: no verdict line
Added missing factorial() primitive to interpreter; diagram uses factorial(n)/(n^n) but the function did not exist so all values were 0. | VERIFIER-ERROR: no verdict line
implement add(dest, src, position, align) picture compositing with Asymptote's align+rectify semantics | VERIFIER-ERROR: no verdict line

Skip unitsize boost when labels dominate tiny geometry (unitScale>=10, naturalMax<=20bp, labelExpansion>=1.25x). Target dims went from 677x858 to 77x87, matching ref 70x64. Remaining SSIM gap is font fallback (librsvg lacks KaTeX_Main, falls back to sans-serif instead of serif bold).

parser treated assignment as non-recursive: 'xmin=ymin=-1.5' consumed only 'xmin=ymin', orphaning '=-1.5' so xmin/ymin stayed at real default 0 and collapsed axes+line draws to origin | VERIFIER-REJECT: diagonal blue line has wrong angle/slope

Added spFills[] and spPens[] palette arrays to installStdlib: AoPS styles.asy spFills[0] is pale cyan/blue (0.77,0.88,0.91) used for region fills in three-semicircles diagrams. Previous attempt placed the palette inside installThreePackage which only runs on 'import three'; moved it next to ASY_COLORS so it is available for 'import styles'. | VERIFIER-REJECT: first label is italic lowercase 'x' instead of capital 'X'
implement transform*real (as implicit real->pair cast); handle \begin{tabular} in label text by flattening rows to \n-separated lines | VERIFIER-OK (high)

Two fixes: (1) extended size-boost threshold to a third tier (unitScale<20 gets minReasonable=80bp) so unitsize(.5cm) diagrams in the c10_L10 family render at legible dimensions; (2) brace() default amplitude now matches Asymptote's bracedefaultratio (0.14*length) instead of hardcoded 0.5 — produces clean curly braces instead of overshooting loops. | VERIFIER-REJECT: overall scale is smaller/more compact in test image; y-axis label is upright instead of italic; x-axis label is upright instead of italic

Diagnosis: Label '35,385.53' with scale(3) renders correctly with 4 arcs below pointing up at digit boundaries and labels 1-4. Structure, colors, arc positions, arrow directions all match reference. Visual differences: (a) KaTeX_Main font renders heavier than Computer Modern used by real LaTeX/TeXeR - unavoidable font substitution artifact affecting ALL diagrams with text; (b) HTX viewBox 200.88x53.4 vs REF aspect translates to HTX PNG being 670x194 vs REF 550x177, wRatio=1.22 causing sizeScore=0.35 penalty. The extra width comes from charWFactor=0.62 in label bbox calc at asy-interp.js:12571, which over-estimates digit width. Approaches tried: analyzed arc/arrow primitives (both work correctly, arrowLen = baseSize*bpCSSPixel = 6 viewBox units matches SVG output); verified font-face CSS embeds KaTeX_Main correctly; confirmed arc direction (0 to -180 CW producing lower semicircle, matches REF). Next attempt should: (1) try a digit-aware width estimator in _estimateTextWidth/charWFactor path for pure-numeric labels like 35,385.53 where digits are narrower than 0.62*fontSize, (2) if aspect ratio improves, re-score family c539_L7 to confirm no regressions from width estimator change, (3) this won't fix font-weight perception differences which are fundamental KaTeX_Main vs CM differences.

markangle radius was converted from bp to user coords assuming bpPerUnit=1 when neither size() nor unitsize() was set; fixed by mirroring renderer's defaultSize=150/maxDim auto-scale logic and including the A,B,C pairs in the bbox estimate so the fix works even when markangle is called before any draw(). | VERIFIER-OK (high)

Boost tgtSize to 300bp (from 200) for small-unitsize (3-10bp) wide-aspect (>=2.5) diagrams; fits horizontally-shifted subgraphs with dense labels. | VERIFIER-REJECT: diagrams are scaled down compared to reference

implemented drawtree module: TreeNode struct, makeNode, draw(TreeNode,pair) dispatch, layout algorithm mirroring adarovsky's drawtree.asy | VERIFIER-REJECT: scale is approximately 3-4× larger than reference

Implement waypoint() (returns relpoint-equivalent for straight segments) and pathticks() (tick marks perpendicular to a path, used by olympiad anglemark congruence indicators). Before fix, waypoint returned undefined so Q collapsed to origin and all derived points (E, F via foot) also collapsed; pathticks did nothing. After fix SSIM 0.665->0.969 and 12 other c57_L7 family IDs also improved. | VERIFIER-REJECT: angle B marker differs: reference has two parallel tick marks, test has arc-style angle marker

Added square(pair,pair), perpendicular-mark draw form, baseline()/PenMargins stubs, and Bar/Bars endpoint rendering. Target 12852 visually matches reference (outer square, tilted diamond, both right-angle marks, bottom/right dimensions with bars, diagonal c). Canary shows major wins (02868 +0.965, 08898 +0.552, 04087 +0.211) with small SSIM drops on a few IDs due to new bar marks / label positioning differences. Target SSIM marginally down (-0.005) due to font/antialiasing noise; structural rendering much improved. | VERIFIER: visual match confirmed despite SSIM 0.6601 < floor
omit opacity on fill/unfill/filldraw to match TeXeR's flattened transparency | VERIFIER-OK (high)
defaultpen(linewidth(n)) carried _lwExplicit into the default pen, making dot() use the per-call diameter formula (tiny dots); clear _lwExplicit/_lwDirect on defaultpen result

defaultpen(linewidth(n)) carried _lwExplicit into the default pen, making dot() use the per-call diameter formula (tiny dots); clear _lwExplicit/_lwDirect on defaultpen result | VERIFIER-REJECT: red dots are darker/more muted than reference

honor Crop flag in xlimits/ylimits to constrain bbox (v2.02) | VERIFIER-REJECT: test image has tick marks on y-axis not present in reference; test image appears vertically compressed or cropped compared to reference

Scale perpendicularmark by picture bbox fraction (0.035 of max extent) instead of sz/28.35 which produced huge 0.353-user-unit marks on size(Ncm) pictures whose user coords don't equal cm. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.8988>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Target render already visually matches reference and scores SSIM 0.8803 (above 0.85 threshold) without any edit this session - prior fixes on the path/cycle interpolation code (commits 8742a5a, 6e5beb4) already resolved the yin-yang S-curve. However, canary shows a pre-existing regression on ID 05886 (baseline 0.7476 -> current 0.5430, delta -0.2045) unrelated to this target. No edit made this session since target is already fixed, so no revert needed. Next attempt: 12952 likely needs no further work - if retried, focus on the pre-existing 05886 regression (a number-line diagram with ArcArrows) which is blocking clean commits across the whole gallery family. A minor visible defect remains on 12952: slight outward bulge at upper-right of black region around 1-2 o'clock where Hobby 3-knot spline through (0,-1)E, (1,0)N, (0,1)W doesn't produce a perfect circular arc, but this is below the commit bar for marginal SSIM gain.
Phong sphere shading + Label Fill propagation; canary -0.20 on 05886 is pre-existing | ACCEPTED: quality=good conf=high
trembling.deform preserves bezier curves (fixes unitcircle-to-octagon); Bars(size) now scales bp to user units

Visual comparison confirms renders are essentially identical. REF is 670x50, HTX is 667x39. Quantitative analysis: all 6 line segments at matching x-positions (midpoints differ by <1.5px: REF 36/152/268/401/517/634 vs HTX 34.5/150.5/266.5/399.5/515.5/631.5), each line 68-70px wide in both. Center dot is 11px wide in both images at x=333-335. Label positions and content match. SSIM gap is caused by (a) 2px vs 3px line thickness (sub-pixel antialiasing at 150dpi), and (b) extra vertical padding in REF (content spans y=1-44 with h=50 canvas; HTX spans y=0-35 with h=39 canvas). The resize-to-common-dimensions step in ssim pipeline stretches HTX vertically ~1.18x relative to REF due to padding mismatch, inflating the pixel-diff. A math student would see these as identical diagrams.

unitsize(40) + 1D path geometry (y=0 for all points) triggered spurious boost gated on fullNat which was label-driven below threshold; added is1DDegenerate check to skip boost when one axis is >=5x smaller than the other | ACCEPTED: quality=good conf=high

Label align with magnitude > 1 (e.g. 3E) was pushing the label box offset proportional to magnitude; fixed by L-infinity normalising the box offset (per Asymptote drawlabel.cc) so magnitude only scales the labelmargin push, not the text-box-half-width offset. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9403>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Default currentprojection changed from orthographic(5,4,2) to perspective(5,4,2) to match Asymptote three.asy default; fixes foreshortening on cubes/3D scenes without explicit currentprojection. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.8906>=0.85, quality=good conf=high
boost unitsize scale when ≥4 co-linear labels crowd horizontally (Riemann-sum x_i tick labels); canary -0.20 on 05886 is pre-existing | ACCEPTED: quality=minor conf=high
honor literal unitsize for cm-scale (>=0.35cm/unit) dot grids with no labels; target +0.305 SSIM, pre-existing canary drift unaffected | VERIFIER-ERROR: parse

implement two-foci ellipse(F1,F2,a) overload from geometry package; canary 'regression' is pre-existing baseline drift on IDs that don't use ellipse | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9894>=0.85, quality=good conf=high
endpoint-tangent default align for Label(text, Relative(r)) on path; also capture align= named arg on draw() | VERIFIER-ERROR: parse
yaxis label defaults to top-endpoint when axis has arrow (no explicit pos/align) | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9117>=0.85, quality=minor conf=high

preserve \, (TeX thin-space) and other backslash-spacing tokens in mixed-mode text segments before escaping for MathJax \text{} | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9175>=0.85, quality=minor conf=high
implement per-picture unitsize(pic, x) scaling | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9946>=0.85, quality=good conf=high
extract diffusepen from material() named args + detect sphere case in revolution() | VERIFIER-ERROR: parse

surface() nx/ny named-arg handling; meshpen wireframe overlay from _grid; drawSimpleAxis for non-Bounds axisType with arrow+label at tip; Arrow3 fn resolution; Label _tag lowercase fix; 3D bounds tracking for path3/projectPathTriples. Canary worst (-0.205 on 05886) is pre-existing baseline drift confirmed via git stash; worstFamilyDelta -0.0005 (no real regression).
Use MJX label measurement for viewBox-overshoot pad in svg-native mode; heuristic was 50% too wide for digit-heavy labels. | ACCEPTED: quality=minor conf=high

Lowered size()-floor boost threshold from 60bp to 5bp so explicit numeric size(N) calls render at literal size like TeXeR. Affects 10 corpus IDs with size(20..50); net SSIM +1.94. The -0.06 family delta on 07662 is a metric artifact (combined score improved ~0 -> 0.889). The -0.20 canary-worst on 05886 is a pre-existing stale baseline unrelated to this edit (verified by stashing the change).
implement intersect2paths as alias for intersectionpoints | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9387>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Detect graph-package xaxis/yaxis idiom and trigger existing unitsize boost when unitScale in [10,20). Fixes the c10_L10 Riemann-sum family (19 diagrams). My change is gated to hasUnitScale, so the negative deltas seen on 00006/00017/00033/00034 are pre-existing baseline drift from earlier commits, not caused by this edit (verified by stashing and re-running). | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.8841>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Implemented \shortstack[pos]{a \ b \ c} expansion in asy-interp.js (added expandShortstackText() near expandMinipageText, wired into evalLabel/evalDot pipelines). The fix is functionally correct: pre-fix the right panel rendered raw red error text '\shortstackimagedipoleaddedoriginaldipoleremoved'; post-fix it correctly shows two stacked lines 'image dipole added' / 'original dipole removed' matching the reference. Verified the same fix improves 08946 from 0.961->0.987 (+0.026) -- shortstack fix is corpus-positive. However, target 03881 SSIM dropped from 0.754->0.672 because the now-correct two-line label changes the bbox/aspect ratio: pre-fix the broken single-line garbled text was wider, post-fix the stacked correct text is taller. The remaining gap is dominated by a systemic font-scaling/bbox issue affecting many corpus diagrams: HTX renders 1645x320 (aspect 5.14) vs REF 757x177 (aspect 4.28); fonts in HTX are too small relative to the diagram coordinate extent compared to TeX's reference rendering. Also tried shifting multi-line label position by -ay_n*totalOffset for proper S-aligned stacking (top-of-block at anchor instead of center) -- this further dropped SSIM to 0.654 because it pushed labels outside prior bbox, so reverted that change. Next attempt should: (1) keep the shortstack expansion (it's right and helps other diagrams), (2) tackle the systemic font/bbox calibration -- possibly by making label-induced bbox extension more compact, or by changing how size(8cm) interacts with label rendering so fonts scale up when the diagram is small relative to the canvas.

isLinear threshold was absolute 0.01; small-radius arcs (r=.025) flattened to L commands. Threshold now scales with chord length. Reported canary/family regressions are pre-existing baseline drift unaffected by this edit (verified by stash test: same SSIM with and without fix on regressing IDs). | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9405>=0.85, quality=good conf=high
prefer last-defined alt on overload tie so function redefinition shadows prior definition | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9944>=0.85, quality=good conf=high
implement intersectionpoints(path3, surface|mesh) for 3D line-plane/face intersection | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9865>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Fixed auto-step picker fallback (n=11 prime fell to step=range), forced major step \!= minor step when subStep explicit, and added autominor sub-ticks for nice major steps. Visual match dramatically improved: y-axis now shows correct labels 2,4,6,8,10 with sub-ticks every 1 unit; x-axis shows just '2' with sub-ticks at 1,3 (matching REF). Target SSIM dropped slightly (0.7213->0.7034) due to layout shift from added labels, not a visual regression. Canary clean (worst -0.0017 within threshold). | ACCEPTED: quality=good conf=high

Skip xaxis nicenum rounding when no Ticks AND no body labels, so bare-arrow plots like Lissajous render with content-tight axis matching yaxis behavior; canary worsts are pre-existing baseline drift not caused by this edit (verified by diff against baseline) | ACCEPTED: quality=good conf=high

is1DDegenerate now requires the small axis to be truly tiny (< 2bp), not merely 5x smaller than the large one. The 5x ratio alone was suppressing the unitsize boost on legitimate wide vector diagrams (wind/plane vectors at unitsize(0.2cm)) where naturalH=9.86bp is small but not degenerate. Canary regression on 05886 is pre-existing baseline drift (verified with git stash); my edit produces identical output for 05886 and all other family members. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9316>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Target is already visually correct and SSIM=0.9874 (well above 0.85 bar) without any edit this session — previous corpus-wide fixes (likely the recent xaxis nicenum/auto-step major-tick changes visible in recent commits) already resolved this 4-leaf rose r=3cos(2t). Phase 1 visual comparison confirms HTX matches REF in shape, color, fill region, axis arrows, and scale. Did not edit asy-interp.js because there is no defect to fix. Did not commit because no code change occurred and the workflow requires asy-interp.js or index.html in the commit. Next attempt should: (a) check render-and-score before editing — if SSIM already > 0.85 just refresh the ssim-results.json baseline and commit that alone, or (b) extend the workflow to allow baseline-only commits when prior corpus fixes have already resolved a target.

Target already renders visually correct in current build (SSIM 0.92, up from stale 0.54 baseline). Digits, four red arcs with arrows, numeric labels 1-4, and overall layout all match the TeXeR reference. Remaining sub-SSIM=1 gap is sub-pixel font hinting/antialiasing on the large '00003.5' text. Prior corpus-wide fixes have already resolved this ID; no asy-interp.js edit needed or made this session.

Already rendering correctly from prior auto-fix commits; only baseline SSIM record was stale. Visual match: text 00003.5 with 4 red arcs tucked under digits and 1-4 labels below. SSIM 0.5445 -> 0.9208. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9208>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

wire CAD module dispatch: preserve NamedArg wrappers in evalMethodCall for sCAD/sCADStatic, fix MakeFreehand positional fallback (pi-counter not pos[1]), bake label perpendicular offset into pos so labelTransform rotation behaves correctly. Canary worst (-0.07) and family worst (-0.28) are pre-existing baseline drift unrelated to this change (verified by git stash diff). | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9514>=0.85, quality=minor conf=high

Visual: HTX render is structurally identical to REF (correct lines, dots, axes, grid, labels). The ONE visible defect is that tick labels render at 12pt while REF uses ~10pt — making HTX labels visibly larger and the canvas ~10% bigger. Root cause: in asy-interp.js:8492-8493, ticks pass through Label('%g',black) where the user's pen has default fontsize 12; the existing 'labelPen.fontsize = labelPen.fontsize || 8' fallback never triggers because 12 is truthy. Tried fixing with 'if (labelPen.fontsize === 12) labelPen.fontsize = N' for N in {8,9,10,11}. ALL lowered SSIM (8:0.580, 9:0.592, 10:0.612, 11:0.640) below baseline 0.674 — even though fontsize 10 visually matches REF best. Reason: trim+resize SSIM is hyper-sensitive to grid/tick anti-aliased edge alignment; smaller labels shift the plot region's pixel layout enough to break alignment that the current 12pt accidentally achieves. Next attempt should NOT touch tick fontsize alone — instead consider (a) fixing fontsize AND adjusting the iterative scale solver (asy-interp.js:16432+) so the plot region grows to match REF's larger-plot+smaller-labels layout, OR (b) leaving fontsize at 12 and tightening label-extent estimation in label bbox code (asy-interp.js:16031+) so canvas shrinks to REF dims without shrinking the actual rendered glyphs (risky: may clip labels).

Already fixed by prior commits; re-render produced matching output (all three colored coordinate planes render correctly). Recorded improved baseline. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9942>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Already fixed by prior commits; render visually matches reference (polar rose r=cos(2theta), correct theta rays, concentric circles, blue/red dot colors). Recording new baseline 0.9247. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9247>=0.85, quality=minor conf=high

Target render already correct due to prior commits. 4x4 binary grid renders with proper serif font, correct cell sizing, and identical digit layout to TeXeR reference. SSIM moved 0.675 -> 0.957 visually validated. No asy-interp.js edits needed. | ACCEPTED: SSIM=0.9572>=0.85, quality=good conf=high

Fixed \frac shorthand parsing and unitsize-boost bbox recompute. Visual match excellent; SSIM gain modest because pixel comparison is dominated by font/anti-aliasing differences. Pre-existing 05886 canary regression not caused by this edit. | ACCEPTED: quality=minor conf=high

Reference is degenerate (504x117 strip with no axes/diagram, just labels piled together) — TeXeR appears to have crashed on the source file's corrupted '\t extnormal{Re}' / '\t extnormal{Im}' text (literal tab + 'extnormal{...}', not '\textnormal{...}') and produced a fallback render with only labels and no graphics. Our HTX render correctly draws a complex plane with axes, ticks, dashed reference lines, and 4 red dots — substantively a much better diagram than the reference. The aspect ratio mismatch alone (htx 682x547 vs ref 504x117 = 4.67x taller) makes SSIM convergence to >=0.85 impossible without deliberately breaking asy-interp.js. DURING THIS SESSION I IDENTIFIED A REAL CORPUS-WIDE BUG in xequals/yequals tick handling: at asy-interp.js lines 9159 and 9230, ticks.size is treated as data units instead of bp. With Size=ticklength (0.1cm = 2.835bp), the tick was drawn 2.835 *units* wide, spanning most of the plot. I prototyped a fix that converts bp to data units via sw/rX (for xequals) and sh/rY (for yequals), matching the _drawTicks logic. The fix improved 08567 SSIM 0.7230 -> 0.7321 (+0.009) and produced a visually MUCH cleaner render (short tick marks, visible dashed lines, all 4 dots clearly placed). Family check: 08568 -0.0003 and 08569 -0.0047, both well within threshold. Canary flagged 05886 (-0.072) but verified that is a stale-canary issue (current ssim-results already at 0.676, canary baseline 0.748 from earlier corpus state). Reverted because target SSIM still under 0.85 due to broken reference. Next attempt: apply the identical fix to xequals/yequals (lines 9159, 9230) — pattern in this attempts notes — and either (a) update the canary baseline for 05886 first, or (b) accept the canary will fail because of stale data, given the family check passes.

Polar coords diagram: source has literal TAB bytes where 	heta was meant. Pre-fix had two issues: (1) evalLabel stripped TAB to empty string -> KaTeX saw \cosheta (unknown command, rendered red); (2) renderLabelMathJaxSVG did not run preprocessLatexForKatex on raw math. Fixed both: TAB->space (TeX terminates control words at whitespace, yielding \cos heta which matches the TeXeR reference exactly, including the literal "heta" rather than theta glyph), and applied preprocessLatexForKatex in MathJax path. Visible defect (red error label) eliminated; SSIM unchanged within noise (-0.001) because the fixed label region is small relative to whole 480x480 canvas which has separate scale-mismatch issue (geometry tiny in center vs reference cropped tightly). Sampled 8 other TAB-bearing files (00020..00041): all delta within +/-0.0003. Canary 05886 -0.072 is stale-canary (verified pre-existing in baseline). Family worst 00021 -0.0002. | ACCEPTED: visible-defect-fix, quality=visual conf=high

Tried reducing slopefield segLen factor from 0.45 to 0.4 in installSlopefieldPackage at asy-interp.js:7707 to better match TeXeR's stubby-arrow style. Target 00263 SSIM improved 0.752->0.770 (+0.018) and visual: arrows visibly shorter, closer to REF appearance. However family check on c10_L22 showed mixed impact across slopefield-using siblings (n=11 IDs use slopefield in this family): 00263/00272/00273 improved (+0.018/+0.051/+0.026) but 00259/00260/00261/00269/00270/00271 regressed slightly (-0.005 to -0.033 each), net family slopefield delta ~-0.018. Tried factors 0.15/0.25/0.3/0.35/0.4/0.42/0.45 - 0.4 was SSIM-optimal for 00263 but no factor avoided mixed family impact. The deeper issue: HTX renders 00263 at lower pxPerUnit than TeXeR (530x556 vs 504x504 for REF), so the fixed-size 6bp arrowhead occupies a larger fraction of cell width in HTX than in REF. Fixing arrowhead/cell ratio uniformly across slopefield diagrams would require coupling segLen scaling to render resolution OR increasing pxPerUnit for slopefield outputs - both touch broader code paths that risk wider canary regression. Note: many family-member baselines in ssim-results.json are stale relative to current code state (e.g. 00273 baseline 0.784, current clean 0.497; 00272 baseline 0.845, current clean 0.579) - these latent drifts predate this attempt and are unrelated to slopefield. Next attempt should consider: (a) scaling segLen by pxPerUnit so visible arrowheads are constant fraction of cell regardless of resolution, (b) increasing axis-arrow size for slopefield-using diagrams to match REF's bigger axis arrowheads, or (c) updating stale family baselines in a separate cleanup PR before tackling per-diagram fixes.

Diagram uses srand(856) + unitrand() to generate 6 random points in [-10,0]^2, then draws lines, circle, and intersection dots based on those points. Current asy-interp.js maps unitrand() to Math.random() (asy-interp.js:7176), so HTX rendering is non-deterministic and SSIM swings between 0.42-0.89 across runs depending on luck. To match TeXeR, unitrand() must produce the SAME sequence as Asymptote's runtime when srand(seed) is called. Tried three deterministic RNGs: (1) routing unitrand through the existing seeded glibc-random emulator at line 7203 -> SSIM 0.623, (2) implementing Mersenne Twister mt19937 -> SSIM 0.868 but visually wrong (parallel lines, no circle), (3) implementing drand48 (Asymptote's documented unitrand backend per runtimebase.in) -> SSIM 0.756, lines+small circle+1 dot but still missing 6 lightgreen dots and full circle from REF. None reproduces TeXeR's exact point sequence; without knowing TeXeR's specific libc/Asymptote build the seed-to-points mapping cannot be reproduced. Secondary defects also present: dot() in evalDot (asy-interp.js:15412) ignores currentpen so 'currentpen=lightgreen; dot(p)' draws black instead of green. Next attempt should: (a) commit the drand48 change as a determinism fix even if it does not raise this specific target's SSIM (it stabilizes SSIM for all 20 srand-using diagrams), (b) separately fix evalDot to honor currentpen, (c) acknowledge that exact pixel match for srand-based diagrams may be infeasible without the upstream RNG identity.
